title: Venture iQube (v0.4)
description: Self-sovereign upload format used by aigentMe to populate ExperienceModel + ExperienceGuide + myCartridge wizard in one shot.

_agent_briefing.audience: An off-platform AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.) helping a user prepare a Venture iQube to upload back into their own aigentMe.
_agent_briefing.what_this_is: A Venture iQube is a structured snapshot of who the operator is, what they're building, and the work in front of them. It travels with them across the AgentiQ platform and populates: (1) their ExperienceModel (the cockpit's strategy + ventures + KPI rails), (2) their ExperienceGuide (the per-venture progressive coaching surface), and (3) their myCartridge wizard (the operator's own first cartridge inside AgentiQ).
_agent_briefing.what_to_produce: A single JSON file that conforms to the schema below. Use the worked example at `examples[0]` as a template and replace every value with the operator's actual content. Keep arrays non-empty where required (ventures, plan horizons, objectives per venture).
system_overview: AgentiQ is a sovereignty-first agent platform. The operator runs metaMe (their guardian), aigentMe (their personal assistant), and one or more Cartridges (their domains of work). aigentMe coordinates with system agents (Aigent Z, Aigent C) and specialists (Marketa, Quill, Know1) to drive each venture forward.

Every operator has an ExperienceModel (the structured plan) and an ExperienceGuide (the coaching surface that walks them through each venture's next-best action). Uploading a Venture iQube populates both, plus optionally a myCartridge configuration if the operator wants to publish a cartridge.

All ingestion is receipt-eligible and zero-knowledge respecting — server-side fields like persona_id never appear in browser-bound responses or chain-bound receipts. The operator's identity stays at T0 (server-internal); only T2 cohort commitments are network-visible.
sovereignty_principles_summary: The operator owns their data. The Venture iQube is theirs; aigentMe ingests it but never resells, syndicates, or trains foundation models on it.

Identity is tiered. T0 (server-internal: personaId, authProfileId, rootDid) NEVER leaves the server. T1 (browser-safe: displayLabel, ownFioHandle) is render-only. T2 (public-network: cohortAliasCommitment) is the only identifier that ever touches a chain receipt.

Access is gated on the spine, not on client UI. Even when this file is uploaded, server-side policy resolves whether each field can be persisted, and the operator's metaMe guardian retains final override over any consequential downstream action.

Receipts are durable. Every state change (intent_queued, artifact_created, artifact_sent, specialist_invoked, etc.) produces a DVN-receipt-eligible orchestration event that is sealed to Bitcoin within a 15-minute audit SLA.

Refresh is daily-or-as-needed. The Venture iQube is a snapshot of where the operator is and where they want to go. Re-upload anytime — the system reconciles by schema version and last-emitted timestamp.
why_safer_than_sharing_with_off_platform: When the operator shares this snapshot with you (an off-platform agent), they share a structured, redactable document — not their live data plane.

You only see what they decide to put in the file. Their identity is opaque to you: there is no personaId, no rootDid, no authProfileId in this schema. The operator's display name and (optional) FIO handle are the only identifiers exposed, both T1-safe.

On upload back into aigentMe, the file passes through a server-side Zod validator (services/iqube/ventureQubeSchema.ts) that enforces every constraint. The runtime resolves the persona from the spine, NOT from any field in this file. Even if you accidentally included a sensitive field, the schema rejects unknown top-level keys.
The myCartridge.ownerPersonaId field is explicitly `never` typed — any attempt to pre-populate it is rejected at parse time. Ownership is spine-resolved.

Compare: pasting strategy notes into a chat with an off-platform agent has no receipt trail, no policy gate, no scoped tool access. Uploading a Venture iQube into aigentMe lands on a receipt-anchored pipeline with cartridge-scoped policy enforcement.
_agent_briefing.refresh_cadence: Re-upload as often as you like — daily standup, weekly review, after a major pivot. Each upload is a snapshot; the system records what changed via DVN receipts. The operator's emittedAt timestamp helps the runtime decide whether to merge or supersede prior plan blocks.

experience_guide_explainer.what_it_is: The ExperienceGuide is the per-venture progressive coaching surface aigentMe renders inside the venture cockpit. It walks the operator through their next-best-action sequence (Brief Me → Move Forward → Engage Specialist → Approve Action → Mark Complete) for each declared objective.
experience_guide_explainer.how_to_help_the_operator_populate_it: The schema doesn't include a literal 'ExperienceGuide' field — the guide is derived at runtime from `ventures[].objectives[]` + `plan` horizons. To maximise coaching quality, encourage the operator to: (1) write `successCriteria[]` for each objective in concrete, observable terms (e.g. 'partner signs LOI', not 'partner is happy'), (2) declare `blockers[]` and `dependencies[]` honestly — the system uses these to suggest the right specialist, (3) split `plan.today` into 1–3 concrete `actions[]` with `owner` + `due`, (4) pick a `specialistHint` per objective (marketa for outreach/PR, quill for content, kn0w1 for research/synthesis).
experience_guide_explainer.guide_personality: The guide is direct. It tells the operator the ONE thing to do next, surfaces blockers, and asks 'do you want me to liaise with X?' before triggering a specialist chain. The richer the success criteria + dependencies, the better the guide's recommendations.

my_cartridge_explainer.what_it_is: myCartridge is the operator's own first cartridge inside AgentiQ — a public-or-private surface they author for an audience (their team, customers, community, or themselves). When the operator wants to ship a cartridge, fill the optional `ventures[].myCartridge` block on the venture that will own it.
my_cartridge_explainer.required_when_present: If `myCartridge.configured` is true, then slug, title, description, purpose, category, visibility, tabs, smartTriad, specialists, activeTab, membershipModel, stateChangeReceipts, and triadNomenclature='v0.2' are all required. See the worked example for shape.
my_cartridge_explainer.tabs_guidance: Always include at least one tab with `primary: true`. Common templates: codex-v1 (knowledge), active-v1 (ops surface), community-v1 (members), pulse-v1 (changelog), wallet-v1 (token economy).

schemaVersion.description: Use v0.4 for new uploads. Earlier versions are accepted for backwards-compatible reads but lack the myCartridge nested block.

emittedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this snapshot was produced. Used by the runtime to reconcile against prior uploads.

displayLabel.description: T1-safe operator name — what aigentMe will address the operator as. NOT the legal name unless the operator wants it shown.

archetype.description: Short self-label. Examples: 'sovereign founder', 'venture studio operator', 'creator-economy builder', 'community steward'.

fioHandle.description: Optional FIO Protocol handle (e.g. 'name@aigentz'). Only the operator's OWN handle. Never include another persona's handle.

headline.description: One-line statement of what the operator is building right now.

thesis.description: Multi-paragraph operating thesis. Why this, why now, who it serves, what success looks like.

blockers.description: Strategic blockers. Be specific — these drive specialist routing.

ventures.description: At least one venture. A 'venture' is a stream of work with its own KPI + objectives. Solo founders often have one. Studios have many.

plan.description: All five horizons are required. Each horizon has a `focus` paragraph and 1–N concrete `actions`.

specialistPreferences.description: Optional map of context → preferred specialist. Example: { 'partner_outreach': 'marketa', 'research_synthesis': 'kn0w1' }.

kpiBoard.description: Optional cross-venture KPI rollup. The cockpit renders this on the home panel.

id.description: Stable slug — keep it consistent across uploads so the runtime can correlate.

cartridgeBindings.description: Which AgentiQ cartridges this venture lives inside (slugs like 'knyt', 'qriptopian', 'mvl').

objectives.description: 1–N objectives. Each becomes a row in the operator's ExperienceGuide.

myCartridge.description: Optional — populate ONLY when the operator wants to ship a cartridge for this venture.

horizon.description: Which plan horizon this maps to. Use one of: today, next24h, next7d, next30d, next90d.

successCriteria.description: Concrete, observable. Drives the ExperienceGuide's 'mark complete' prompt.

specialistHint.description: Suggested specialist for this objective. One of: marketa, quill, kn0w1, aigent-z, aigent-c, aigent-nakamoto, moneypenny, metaye.

focus.description: One-paragraph statement of what matters in this horizon.

ventureId.description: Must reference a ventures[].id declared above.

available.description: Free tier caps available specialists at 3 — payment-gated above that. Suggest the 3 most aligned with the venture's blockers.

strategy.headline: Launch a creator-owned distribution network for independent musicians in Q3.
strategy.thesis: Independent musicians currently lose 70% of streaming revenue to platform intermediaries. A creator-owned distribution co-op, anchored by a token-incentivised superfan tier, can flip the economics. Phase 1: 500-artist closed beta with three flagship labels. Phase 2: open marketplace with cross-platform syndication. Phase 3: rights tokenisation.
blockers: need a launch partner with existing artist roster

ventures.name: Creator Co-op
ventures.northStarKpi: 500 artists onboarded by end of Q3

objectives.title: Secure one flagship label as launch partner
objectives.summary: Need an established indie label to co-sign the beta and bring their artist roster.

ventures.notes: Founder has a personal relationship with Untitled Records' A&R lead; warm intro arranged for next week.

myCartridge.title: Creator Co-op
myCartridge.description: Creator-owned distribution + superfan tier for independent musicians.
myCartridge.purpose: Surface the co-op's founding cohort, distribution rails, and contributor compensation model. Doubles as the project's public canon.

copilot.promptContext: You are the Creator Co-op cartridge copilot. Help founding members navigate distribution, rights, and contributor compensation. Defer to Marketa on outreach, Quill on copy, Know1 on rights research.

today.focus: Lock the Untitled Records intro call. Get the pitch deck v2 in shape.

actions.title: Send confirmation email to Untitled A&R
Final pass on pitch deck v2

next24h.focus: Have the intro call. Send same-day follow-up with deck.

next7d.focus: Convert the intro call into a term-sheet conversation.

next30d.focus: Signed launch partner. Beta cohort confirmed.

next90d.focus: Beta live with first 100 artists. First distribution window run.

kpiBoard.name: Artists onboarded
kpiBoard.metric: count of artists with signed distribution agreement
